dealSummary: In her research, the majority of patients with neurodegenerative conditions said they did not want an implant or would consider it only if their symptoms worsened. There are over 90 million stroke survivors globally, approximately 65% of whom will experience motor disability six months post-stroke. More than 11 million people have Parkinson's, and 20 to 25 million people globally have non-cataract blindness. The addressable market for BCIs extends to young, healthy Americans to improve cognitive function and memory, according to some VCs and entrepreneurs. "It becomes a device a lot like a smartphone, where it's very common, and everyone has it," said D. Scott Phoenix, a partner at Fifty Years. Similarly, some investors believe that for humans to keep up with the pace of AI intelligence, they'll need to augment human cognition with neurotechnology. But even some risk-takers in venture capital are staying away altogether. Obvious Ventures, which has invested in several AI healthtech and biotech plays, has passed, in part due to safety concerns. Even the category's biggest boosters are cautiously optimistic. "People aren't going to just go to their Apple store and want a brain chip," said Abdo John Hajj, managing partner at Type One Ventures. "Commercialization in the short term looks like the world's best-in-class healthcare organizations."Still, that's not stopping some VCs from envisioning that neurotech could someday enable digital consciousness that lives on after the body gives out. But for a pragmatic investor, the realities of user acquisition are less fantastical.
